chunks.text: Education is a basic right of every person.
The United Nations has declared the right to education as one of the universal human rights.
the opportunity for education differs greatly according to the country one is born in and the income of one's family.
There are still tens of millions of children worldwide who cannot attend school.
Even among developed countries, there are large gaps in the quality and access of education.
The economic condition of a family greatly shapes a child's future career.
Education is not only the passing on of knowledge.
It is a process by which an individual forms his identity and grows into a responsible member of society.
Many countries, through scholarships and the reduction of tuition
are trying to open the chance of higher education to more young people.
The reform proceeds slowly, but it does proceed.
To create a truly equal society
every child must be given the same opportunity for education.
Only when education is equal does the gap in society begin to close.
